item.description: Iran and Israel exchanged strikes for the first time since the April ceasefire as tensions rise once again in the Middle East. After Iran’s attack, President Donald Trump said he would ask Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu "not to retaliate" and that he will have to accept any deal the U.S. negotiates with Iran, saying, “He won’t have any choice…I call all the shots.” NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Trump Tells Israel Not to Strike Iran, Saying He 'Calls the Shots'
media:content.media:description: Iran and Israel exchanged strikes for the first time since the April ceasefire as tensions rise once again in the Middle East. After Iran’s attack, President Donald Trump said he would ask Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu "not to retaliate" and that he will have to accept any deal the U.S. negotiates with Iran, saying, “He won’t have any choice…I call all the shots.” NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

item.title: Trump Tells Israel Not to Strike Iran, Saying He 'Calls the Shots'

item.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump addresses the economy amid the war with Iran and says Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh is “fantastic,” adding, “I want him to do whatever he wants” when it comes to setting interest rates.

media:content.media:title: Trump on new Fed Chair Warsh: ‘I don’t want to have a big influence on him’
media:content.media:description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump addresses the economy amid the war with Iran and says Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh is “fantastic,” adding, “I want him to do whatever he wants” when it comes to setting interest rates.

item.title: Trump on new Fed Chair Warsh: ‘I don’t want to have a big influence on him’

item.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump says “the weaponization fund is a great idea” and if Republicans “don't get it approved, I'd be disappointed.” Asked whether anyone who attacked police officers on January 6 should get taxpayer money, Trump says, “I wouldn't be inclined to say so, but I have to see it.”

media:content.media:title: Trump says ‘I’d pay’ anti-weaponization fund applicants ‘the kind of money they deserve’
media:content.media:description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump says “the weaponization fund is a great idea” and if Republicans “don't get it approved, I'd be disappointed.” Asked whether anyone who attacked police officers on January 6 should get taxpayer money, Trump says, “I wouldn't be inclined to say so, but I have to see it.”

item.title: Trump says ‘I’d pay’ anti-weaponization fund applicants ‘the kind of money they deserve’

item.description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to share key takeaways her exclusive interview with President Donald Trump about the war in Iran, its impact on the economy and his claims of voter fraud in California and the 2020 election. Kristen says, “I pressed him on his promise not to get the United States engaged in foreign wars. He said ‘This is not going to be a forever war.’ He insists it’s not a quagmire.”

media:content.media:title: Trump on Pressure to End Iran War With Peace Deal
media:content.media:description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to share key takeaways her exclusive interview with President Donald Trump about the war in Iran, its impact on the economy and his claims of voter fraud in California and the 2020 election. Kristen says, “I pressed him on his promise not to get the United States engaged in foreign wars. He said ‘This is not going to be a forever war.’ He insists it’s not a quagmire.”

item.title: Trump on Pressure to End Iran War With Peace Deal

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Gregg Popovich coached San Antonio to five NBA titles, before stepping aside in 2025 after he suffered a stroke. He’s still involved, though, giving advice and guidance behind the scenes.

media:content.media:text: San Antonio Spurs head coach Gregg Popovich, right, talks with center Victor Wembanyama (1) during the first half of an NBA basketball game against the Los Angeles Lakers in San Antonio, Wednesday, Dec. 13, 2023.
media:content.media:description: San Antonio Spurs head coach Gregg Popovich talks with Victor Wembanyama during an NBA game against the Los Angeles Lakers on Dec. 13, 2023.

item.title: The Spurs’ secret weapon this postseason? A former coach offering pearls of wisdom

item.description: Votes are still being counted in the closely-watched primary races in California, including for mayor of Los Angeles. Conservative reality TV star Spencer Pratt has dropped to third place behind current democratic Mayor Karen Bass, who is heading to the runoff, and progressive city councilwoman Nithya Raman. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Spencer Pratt Drops to Third Place in LA Mayor’s Race
media:content.media:description: Votes are still being counted in the closely-watched primary races in California, including for mayor of Los Angeles. Conservative reality TV star Spencer Pratt has dropped to third place behind current democratic Mayor Karen Bass, who is heading to the runoff, and progressive city councilwoman Nithya Raman. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

item.title: Spencer Pratt Drops to Third Place in LA Mayor’s Race

item.description: North Korea hosted Chinese President Xi Jinping for the first time in seven years as the countries agree to expand cooperation in a range of areas from trade to agriculture. NBC News’ Janis Mackey Frayer reports.

media:content.media:title: Chinese President Xi visits North Korea
media:content.media:description: North Korea hosted Chinese President Xi Jinping for the first time in seven years as the countries agree to expand cooperation in a range of areas from trade to agriculture. NBC News’ Janis Mackey Frayer reports.

item.title: Chinese President Xi visits North Korea

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Idris Elba dismissed talk of him playing James Bond, saying certain audiences won’t accept a Black man in the role and that the character shouldn’t be “woke.”

media:content.media:text: Idris Elba at the Venice International Film Festival on September 2, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Idris Elba at the Venice International Film Festival in September.

item.title: Idris Elba says James Bond rumors not ‘realistic,’ some fans won’t accept ‘Black male’ in role

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: A California McDonald’s employee suffered burns across his face, neck, arm and back after his colleague allegedly threw hot oil on him.

media:content.media:text: Jacob Smith recovers in the UC Davis ICU burn unit after police say a coworker at a McDonald's in Yuba City, Calif., threw hot liquid on him.
media:content.media:description: Jacob Smith recovers in the UC Davis ICU burn unit after police say a coworker at a McDonald's in Yuba City, Calif., threw hot liquid on him.

item.title: McDonald’s worker suffers severe burns on face and body after coworker throws hot oil on him

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Gregg Popovich coached San Antonio to five NBA titles, before stepping aside in 2025 after he suffered a stroke. He’s still involved, though, giving advice and guidance behind the scenes.

media:content.media:text: San Antonio Spurs head coach Gregg Popovich, right, talks with center Victor Wembanyama (1) during the first half of an NBA basketball game against the Los Angeles Lakers in San Antonio, Wednesday, Dec. 13, 2023.
media:content.media:description: San Antonio Spurs head coach Gregg Popovich talks with Victor Wembanyama during an NBA game against the Los Angeles Lakers on Dec. 13, 2023.

item.title: The Spurs’ secret weapon this postseason? A former coach offering pearls of wisdom

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Idris Elba dismissed talk of him playing James Bond, saying certain audiences won’t accept a Black man in the role and that the character shouldn’t be “woke.”

media:content.media:text: Idris Elba at the Venice International Film Festival on September 2, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Idris Elba at the Venice International Film Festival in September.

item.title: Idris Elba says James Bond rumors not ‘realistic,’ some fans won’t accept ‘Black male’ in role

item.dc:creator: Stella Kim
Mithil Aggarwal
item.description: Kim Jong Un offered China’s president a grand welcome Monday.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-CHINA-DEFENCE-ANNIVERSARY-WWII-PARADE
media:content.media:description: In this pool photograph distributed by the Russian state agency Sputnik, (L-R) Russia's President Vladimir Putin walks with China's President Xi Jinping and North Korea's leader Kim Jong Un before a military parade marking the 80th anniversary of victory over Japan and the end of World War II, in Beijing's Tiananmen Square on September 3, 2025.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-NKOREA-CHINA-DIPLOMACY
media:content.media:description: The national flags of North Korea and China are displayed on a street in Pyongyang on Monday.

media:content.media:text: North Korea
media:content.media:description: Kim Jong Un, front right, visits a new facility to produce nuclear material at an undisclosed place in North Korea, on Wednesday.

item.title: Kim Jong Un hosts Xi Jinping from a position of rare strength

item.dc:creator: Raquel Coronell Uribe
Gary Grumbach
item.description: Plaintiffs allege the June 14 event violates National Park Service regulations and benefits Trump and his allies.

media:content.media:text: A large arch with the colors of the American flag rises in front of the White House.
media:content.media:description: The suit alleges that even though the UFC claims it is “eating” the costs of the event and isn’t selling tickets, “the event will likely be profitable for the UFC and its partners.”

item.title: Federal lawsuit aims to stop UFC event on the White House South Lawn

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
Nollaig O'Connor
item.description: Cellphone video captured a spectacular, pre-Fourth of July display of fireworks on a Tennessee highway Saturday evening as a trailer carrying the incendiary haul erupted, authorities said

media:content.media:text: Explosions send sparks and smoke from a white truck on an interstate
media:content.media:description: A still taken from video shows intense fireworks explosions from a trailer on TN Interstate, June 6, 2026.

item.title: Trailer carrying fireworks ignites in spectacular display on Tennessee highway

item.dc:creator: Tom Winter
Marlene Lenthang
item.description: Six people were injured in a knife attack at New York City’s Penn Station on Sunday, authorities said

media:content.media:text: Fire Department vehicles with emergency lights flashing in Manhattan at dusk.
media:content.media:description: Emergency vehicles outside Penn Station in Manhattan on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: Four law enforcement agents outside Penn Station at dusk.
media:content.media:description: Emergency vehicles outside Penn Station in Manhattan on Sunday.

item.title: 6 injured in knife attack at New York City’s Penn Station

item.dc:creator: Scott Bland
item.description: Los Angeles City Council member Nithya Raman, a Democrat, has taken a narrow lead for second place over former reality TV star Spencer Pratt, a registered Republican, in the race to take on Mayor Karen Bass in a runoff this fall.

media:content.media:text: Spencer Pratt Nithya Raman and Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass.
media:content.media:description: Nithya Raman, left, and Spencer Pratt, center, are competing in the race against Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass.

item.title: Nithya Raman leaps past Spencer Pratt in tight race to make L.A. mayoral runoff

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: We’re tracking the best early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals, including discounts on tech, home, beauty, kitchen products and more before the four-day sale begins.

media:content.media:text: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.
media:content.media:description: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.

media:content.media:text: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.
media:content.media:description: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.

item.title: The 51+ best early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals you can shop now: Bissell, Le Creuset, Levi’s, and more

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: Le Creuset Dutch ovens, stainless steel pans, nonstick ceramic skillets and more are up to 40% off at the brand’s Summer Cookware Event.

media:content.media:text: Le Creuset’s cookware is known for its sturdiness and excellent heat retention.
media:content.media:description: Le Creuset’s cookware is known for its sturdiness and excellent heat retention.

item.title: Le Creuset Summer Sale: Save $170 on Dutch ovens and up to 40% on nonstick pans and more

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening from June 23 to 26, and only Prime members get access. Here’s what you need to know and what’s new about the sale this year.

media:content.media:text: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.
media:content.media:description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.

media:content.media:text: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.
media:content.media:description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.

item.title: When is Amazon Prime Day 2026? The official dates, plus more about the 4-day sale

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: Target just announced its dates for Target Circle Week also called Target Circle Deal Days. The sale event will take place fromTuesday, June 23 to Friday, June 26.

media:content.media:text: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.
media:content.media:description: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.

media:content.media:text: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.
media:content.media:description: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.

item.title: Target just announced dates for Target Circle Week: Here’s everything you should know

Marlene Lenthang

media:content.media:text: Fire Department vehicles with emergency lights flashing in Manhattan at dusk.

item.title: 6 injured in knife attack at New York City’s Penn Station

item.description: A California McDonald’s employee suffered burns across his face, neck, arm and back after his colleague allegedly threw hot oil on him.

media:content.media:text: Jacob Smith recovers in the UC Davis ICU burn unit after police say a coworker at a McDonald's in Yuba City, Calif., threw hot liquid on him.
media:content.media:description: Jacob Smith recovers in the UC Davis ICU burn unit after police say a coworker at a McDonald's in Yuba City, Calif., threw hot liquid on him.

item.title: McDonald’s worker suffers severe burns on face and body after coworker throws hot oil on him

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: The trio allegedly paid more than $2,000 to purchase drones purportedly to be used to kill U.S. service members overseas.

media:content.media:text: The exterior of a government building with the DOJ seal on its facade.
media:content.media:description: Three U.S. citizens were arrested Friday and accused of plotting to support ISIS following an investigation by the FBI Joint Terrorism Task Force.

item.title: Three U.S. men arrested on charges of plotting to support ISIS

item.description: Dramatic video shows a pickup truck carrying a trailer full of fireworks exploding on Tennessee highway.

media:content.media:title: Pickup truck carrying trailer full of fireworks explodes on Tennessee highway
media:content.media:description: Dramatic video shows a pickup truck carrying a trailer full of fireworks exploding on Tennessee highway.

item.title: Pickup truck carrying trailer full of fireworks explodes on Tennessee highway
